datafield.subfield: Est in 10th cent. Very rich Library in Greek and Slavic MSS, 17.000 slavic and 3.000 greek rare books. Library holds 102 Byzantine music MSS, rare books on Byzantine music.

The monastery is situated between the monasteries of Great Lavra and Iviron, on a slope by the sea and is dedicated to St Peter and St Paul. Inside the fortified enclosure, there is the Katholikon of athonite type, built in mid 16th c. and decorated with frescoes in the early 18th c. The monastery has 7 chapels, 4 Kellia in Karyes, and 14 Kellia in the forest to the S.W. of it. The monastery is mentioned in documents of 1018 and 1087. In the 13th c. the monastery is entirely ruined and rebuilt by the emperors Andronic II and Ioannis V Paleologos. Afterwards the monastery is attacked by Latins and pirates. In the 16th c. it is completely destroyed and rebuilt with the financial aid of rulers of Moldavia and Vlachia. In the monastery, there are many portable icons, holy heirlooms and ecclesiastical vessels. The library contains 279 manuscripts, among them several music ones, and about 2,500 books.
Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, Library catalog
Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, Library catalog

The Holy Monastery of Chilandari stands on the north-eastern side of Athos. Some maintain that its name comes in all probability from the founder of an older monastic house on the site, while others derive the name from the word 'chelandion' - a kind of Byzantine ship. Monastery was founded in the 12th century by Stefan Nemanja, Prince of Serbia, and his son Rastko, who subsequently became monks, taking the names of Symeon and Sabbas. In 1198, the Serbian possession of the Monastery was ratified by a chrysobull of the Emperor Alexius III. Gradually many other smaller historic monasteries, such as those of Zygou, of St Basil, of Komitissas, of Kalyka, of Paparnikion, of Omologetou, and of Strovilaias were annexed and included within the boundaries of Chilandari's property.The library contains 181 Greek and 809 Slavonic codices (incl music mss), at least 20,000 printed books, many of them of byzantine music, of which 3,000 are in Greek, as well as 400 documents, in various languages.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-AOse.

Library is well organised. It houses 804 manuscript codices (125 parchments, 3 palimpsests, 641 in paper, 11 in cotton) and 27 scrolls (22 parchments and 5 in paper). The majority of these manuscripts are theological and liturgical texts, such as gospels, praxapostoli, martyrologies, patristic texts (orations, speeches, ascetic and neptic discourse), liturgical books, handwritten sheet music for ecclesiastical pieces, as well as the works of the classics. 58 of the collection’s codices and scrolls are decorated with miniatures as well as ornate headpieces and historiated initials.

Library's closed access collection comprises 950 codices (bound volumes of manuscripts) of parchment and paper from the ninth century, some of which are illustrated. It also houses more than 5200 printed books ranging from 1500 to 1890 covering topics on Literature, Grammar, European and World History, Travel, Medicine, Art, Philosophy and Theology. Many mmss and books on byzantine music.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-AOk.

The Greatest Lavra is the oldest monastery on the Holy Mountain. It was founded in 963 by St. Athanasius the Athonite, who received financial assistance from the emperors Nicephorus Phocas and John Tzimisces for its construction. Its library houses the largest manuscripts collection than any other Monastery in Mt Athos, 2,242 in all, dating back as far as the tenth century and including a hundred in Slavonic One of the oldest is a parchment Gospel book (992) written in minuscule, adorned with eleven arch-framed decorative designs showing the concordance of the Gospels. A good collection of early prints is housed in the Library, 220 Byzantine music MSS, rare books on Byzantine music.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-AOml.
Σκλαβενίτης,Τριαντάφυλλος Ε. Η Βιβλιοθήκη Εντύπων της Μονής Μεγίστης Λαύρας του Άθω. Μνήμων, 11, 83–122 (2003). https://doi.org/10.12681/mnimon.129

Stavronikita is the smallest of all athonite monasteries. The catholicon of the monastery is dedicated to Saint Nicholas and is the smallest catholicon among its other athonite counterparts. It was built during the 16th century and is decorated with frescoes and an iconostasis by the famous icon-painter Theophanes of Crete and his son Symeon. The monastery's refectory is located on the upper floor at the southern side of the complex and bears some important iconographies. The Library and the treasury houses a collection of 171 manuscripts, out of which 58 are written on parchment. Some of the manuscripts bear notable iconography and decoration. Byzantine music mss, among them of Synesios Stavronikitianos and Matthaios Vatopaidinos. Books on byzantine music of the 19th century.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-AOsta.

Μαυροπούλου-Τσιούμη, Χρυσάνθη , Γαλαβάρης, Γιώργος. Holy Stavroniketa Monastery: Illustrated Manuscripts From 10th to 17th Century = Ιερά Μονή Σταυρονικήτα : εικονογραφημένα χειρόγραφα από τον 10o έως τον 17o αιώνα. Mount Athos: Holy Stavroniketa Monastery, 2007.
Est in 17th cent. Library of the Skete contains 200 MSS, 3 parchment cod. Byzantine music MSS, rare books on Byzantine music.
Est. in 10th cent. Library holds 409 MSS and 4.000 rare books. 131 Byzantine music MSS, rare books on Byzantine music.

Founded in 1939 as a part of the National Theatre, the National Opera became independent in 1944. The Library has been functioning since 1939 and is a working collection for the opera company. There are full scores and parts of many operas and operettas. Many have unpublished Greek translations written in, some only in the chorus parts. The full scores of Greek operas and operettas are mostly in MSS. There is a vast collection of piano-vocal scores, many of which are Greek works in MSS. A collection of unpublished librettos include some Greek translations of foreign works. The audiovisual collection consists mostly of works acquired for preparation of first performances at the National Opera. In addition to vocal scores there are also ballet scores and parts. A full set of programs of past performances is in the Library, but annals and press clippings are found in the administrations offices of the Opera. There are catalogues and indexes. Collection is housed in the new Opera House, Stavros Niarchos Foundation Cultural Centre. The Library is open to qualified scholars.
Third Programme of Greek Radio, Library

Airs on September 19, 1954, with the author's initiative of Dionysios Romas, two years after the founding of the Second Program. The station then had a neglected medium wave transmitter, and transmitting on an intra-daily schedule. The actual story, however, began during the dictatorship and afterwards, when the great composer Manos Hatzidakis became director. With a program that attracts the interest of people of all ages, even children the Third Programme gained popularity. Addressing the classical music and various cultural activities, the Third Programme, is the only such radio in Greece, having loyal audience.

The Third Programme (Greek: Τρίτο Πρόγραμμα, Trito Programma) is the third public radio station of Greece's state broadcaster, ERT. The station's main program broadcasts focused on culture and classical music, and enjoyed great prosperity during the period when it was headed by Manos Hatzidakis. Since February 2016, the station chief is Dionysis Mallouchos.
The music library holds about 15,000 works titles - scores and parts. These are printed publications and manuscripts of musical works from the late 19th century to the present.

Structure - Functions Department of Preservation, Digitization and Documentation. Key competencies are: • maintenance and restoration of the original archive material • digitization of each physical medium of our Archive (video, photo, film, radio and paper material) • Quality control and restoration of the generated digital item • documentation of the physical and digital records of the Archive: a systematic and standardized way of recording all the information that accompanies each item (object) of the File • Keeping, preserving and highlighting the collections of museum objects, from the technological evolution of radio and television in Greece (receivers, projectors, transmitters, loudspeakers, consoles, muvioli etc)

Department of Archives-Museum Management Responsibilities are: • recording, sorting, archiving of all archives and museum objects of ERT SA • Acquisition of new broadcasting material • Responsibility to manage archival material within and outside ERT SA • management of requests from third parties for the concession of material, with or without payment, as well as policy formulation, in cooperation with other ERT service units, for the distribution of the audiovisual material to third parties for commercial purposes • provision of programs and archives in ERT SA broadcasting productions.

The Gennadius Library, which opened its doors in 1926 to house the important donation of 26,000 volumes of books by diplomat and book-knot John Gennadius, today holds a unique research collection of more than 138,000 volumes of books as well as rare bindings, archives, manuscripts and works of art that refer to the Greek tradition and neighboring cultures. The Library is housed in a building of particular architectural significance, right across from the central building of the American School of Classical Studies. Gennadius has become an internationally recognized center of study of Greek history, literature and art from antiquity to modern times. It also participates in research projects, as well as digitization programs. Combined with its function as a library and research center, "Gennadius" actively participates in the cultural life of Athens with lectures, seminars, concerts, exhibitions and publications.
Rare and Special collections made up of manuscript codices, watercolors, engravings, historical maps, pamphlets, and various memorabilia still follows Gennadius’ own cataloging arrangement.

New acquisitions are guided by a carefully-formulated Acquisitions Policy, inspired by the founding collection and complementing the collections of the Blegen Library across the street, which are focused on earlier periods of Greek history and prehistory.
The research collection is being classified according to the Library of Congress classification system and will be accessible on open stacks in September 2019.

The Gennadius Library is a non-circulating, closed stacks library. No book or document may leave the library. We welcome all serious researchers to use the Library and its collections. First-time readers must apply for a library card. To avoid disappointment when you arrive at the library, and to find out what documents you need to bring to obtain this, please contact the desk attendant at: gen_recep@ascsa.edu.gr All readers must follow the instructions of the desk attendants and abide by the rules and regulations of the Reading Room. Please consult the reading room rules prior to visiting the library. Contact Tel. +30-210-7210536 Hours Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday & Friday - 9:00 to 17:00 Thursday - 9:00 to 20:00 Saturday - 9:00 to 14:00
Mousseio ke Kendro Meletis Ellinikou Theatrou
Kentro Mikrasiatikon Erevnōn kai Spoudōn, Vivliothēkē
Center for Asia Minor Studies, Library
Tmēma Mousikōn Spoudōn, Ethniko Kapodistriako Pane pistēmio Athenon, Vivliothēkē
School of Musical Studies, National Kapodistrian University of Athens, Library

The National Library of Greece was established by the decree, issued on 15 May 1832 in Nafplio, under the name "Public Library" and with the director Gennadios, which was titled "Epistatos". In 1834 the National Library comes to Athens, which is the capital of the Greek state, and its collection grows rapidly, thanks not only to the markets but also to the donations of books by individuals. Ιn 1842 it is consolidated with the Library of the University of Athens and is housed at the University. In 1866, with a royal decree, the two libraries are merged and the new entity will be named "National Library of Greece". In 1903, the National Library of Greece was transferred from the oldest building of the University to Vallianeio building, where it was housed continuously until 2017. In 2018 the National Library of Greece moves to the new phase of its operation at the premises of the Stavros Foundation Cultural Center Niarchos.

The National Library of Greece holds one of the largest collections of Greek Manuscripts internationally. In particular, it has a collection of approximately 4,500 manuscripts, parchments, charts, and scrolls of Byzantine music, dated from the 9th to 19th centuries and 254 Byzantine and post-Byzantine music MSS date from the 12th to the 20th centuries. The collections includes books of Greek folk music, church music, method books (in Greek), music periodicals, collections of various kinds of songs, music scores (exceptionally strong in 20th cent ecclesiastical MSS, biographies, books on music in the Ionian islands, photographs, over 75 pamphlets relating to Philharmonic Societies all over Greece, newspaper clippings, magnetic tapes, and a small collection of Greek popular music (including 52 entries under Mikis Theodorakis). Other materials of special interest are the MSS of the 20th cent composer and pedagogue John A. Papaioannou, and a small collection of MSS written and dedicated to the members of the 19th cent Greek royal family.

Its mission is to identify, collect, organize, describe and preserve in the past the scientific and cultural evidence created in Greece or internationally and related to Hellenism throughout its time, offering open and equal access to any interested person.
Syllogos Hoi Philoi tēs Mousikēs, Megalē Mousikē Bibliothēkē tēs Hellados Lilian Voudourē
Syllogos Hoi Philoi tēs Mousikēs, Megalē, Mousikē Bibliothēkē tēs Hellados Lilian, Voudourē, Vas. Sofias & Kokkali 1, 11521 Athēnai, Greece
The Friends of Music Society, Music Library of Greece Lilian Voudouri
Theatrikon Mouseion tes Hetaireias Hellēnōn Theatrikōn Syngrapheōn
Theatrical Museum of the Society of Greek Playwrights
Dēmosia Kentrikē Historikē Vivliothēkē Chiou “Adamantios Koraēs”
Δημόσια Κεντρική Ιστορική Βιβλιοθήκη Χίου «Αδαμάντιος Κοραής»
Public Central Historic Library of Chios “Adamantios Koraēs"

The Koraes Library is one of the biggest libraries in Greece. Founded in 1792, it was originally an annex to the Great School of Chios. The latter currently houses the 1st Middle School of Chios. One of the most importrant of the oldest provincial libraries and popularly known as the Vivliothēkē Chiou “Korais”. It bears the name of the famous Greek humanist, patriot, and language reformer who was a tireless campaigner for the establishment of libraries in Greece before and after the revolution of 1821. He was a particular friend of Chios and, aside from donating his own personal collection to the library there, was instrumental in obtaining gifts of books from scholars in northern Europe. Other important gifts and bequests have come from prominent Greeks and natives of Chios. Of the ca. 180.000 vols. now in the library there are many rare MSS and imprints, including 17 incunabula. Books and MSS of Byzantine Music have been acquired from monasteries and churches of Chios. There are ca 80 books on Byzantine Music and 30 music MSS from the 18th and 19th centuries.
Library of the Metropolis of Kastoria
The small library of the Metropolis is housed in the main building of the Diocese, next to the Metropolis church and includes 7 byzantine music mss.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-KA.
Politēs, Linos.Συνοπτική αναγραφή χειρογράφων Ελληνικών συλλογών. Thessalonikē: Hetairia Makedonikōn Spoudōn,1976.
Wellesz, Egon, Velimirovic, Milos. Studies in Easterh Chant: Vol. III. London: Oxford University Press, 1973

Est 1836, this Society has a Library of over 20.000 vols which is strong in MSS and rare books related to the Greek revolution. Music materials include a fine collection of MSS, published music, and correspondence of Nikolaos Mantzaros among which is the first printing of the Greek National Anthem (London 1873) as well as later additions. There are also MSS and printed score of other Corfu composers and composers associated with Corfu. Many clippings from newspapers and periodicals documents music of Corfu and the rest of Greece. Of wider musical interest is a collection of 132 Italian opera librettos, most of which were published on Corfu from 1823 to about 1885 for performances of Italian touring troupes. From 1775 Corfu had been a regular stop for all these itinerant companies, and the librettos (including works of Bellini, Donizetti, and others) both document the performances and include cast lists as well. The Society sponsors lectures and concerts and publishes an important bulletin of literary and scholarly note “Deltion Anagnostikēs Hetaireias Kerkyras”. The Library is open to the public Mon-Sat.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-Kae.
Κανονισμός της Αναγνωστικής Εταιρείας Κερκύρας. Kerkyra: [s.n.], 1848
Αναγνωστική Εταιρία Κέρκυρας. Kerkyra: Anagnōstikē Hetaireia Kerkyras, 2006
Society of Corfu Studies-Solomos Museum. Library

The Society of Corfu Studies was founded in 1940 and operates ever since to support the study of history, folklore as well as the overall intellectual and art production of the Ionian Islands. This private intellectual society operates a Mouseio Solomou (Solomos Museum) as well as a centre for creative studies with a Library devoted to Modern Greek Studies, There are more than 200 music MSS and printed scores related exclusively to musical settings of the works of the poet Dionysios Solomos and other Greek composers. The Library is open to the public
The former RISM library siglum was GR-Keks.
Online map of music collections in Greece. AMA WG GR IAML
Ionio Panepistimio, Ergastērio Hellēnikēs Mousikēs, Vivliothēkē
Ionian University, Dept. of Music Studies, Hellenic Music Research Lab Library
Ιόνιο Πανεπιστήμιο, Εργαστήριο Ελληνικής Μουσικής, Βιβλιοθήκη
Ionian University, Hellenic Music Research Lab Library

The Hellenic Music Research Lab was founded in 2000 and its library operates since 2008. It serves the study of history, aesthetics, theory, analysis, and the promotion of Hellenic Music (ancient, medieval and contemporary) with a special emphasis on its scholarly forms.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-Kms.
Ionian University Library and Information Online Public Access Catalog

Est 1938 as a music and dramatic school for young people, the Society maintains a music library for use of its members and students. It is a primarily working collection of band music. Aside from published scores and parts of European music there is an important collection of MS parts of arrangements and original works by European and Greek composers. Many of the MSS are gifts from copyists/arrangers such as Spyros Argalias and Spyros Belōnēs. The Navy Band has also given music to the Society. In addition to serious music there are also Greek folk dances and “kantades”, folk songs typical of the Ionian Islands. Not generally open to the public and no regular hours of operation, but application for admission may be made directly to the Society.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-Kpg.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-Kpk.
Rōmanou, Kaitē. «Ένα αρχείο ‘Κρητικής Μουσικής’ στη Φιλαρμονική Εταιρεία Κερκύρας». Μουσικολογία 12-13. (2000): 175-188.

Papadatou, Marina Ch. «Η φιλαρμονική εταιρεία Κέρκυρας από το έτος συστάσεως της 1840 μέχρι τα τέλη του 19ου αιώνα». MA Thesis. Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, 1993. https://sophia.mus.auth.gr/xmlui/handle/123456789/779
Kardamēs, Kōstas. Έξι μελέτες για τη φιλαρμονική εταιρεία Κέρκυρας. Kerkyra: Philarmonikē Hetaireia Kerkyras, 2010.

Kardamēs, Kōstas.«Το αρχείο της Φιλαρμονικής Εταιρείας Κερκύρας: Ιστορική αναδρομή και πολιτικές ανάδειξης». 1st Conference of Greek Music Libraries, 21-22 April 2017, Athens. Edited by Aris Bazmadelis, Valia Vraka, Vera Kriezi, Christina Theodorika, Alexandros Charkiolakis. Athēna: Herodotos, [u.p].

The core of the founders of "Mantzaros", comes from a socialist group, which was active in Corfu in early 1890. Before June in 1890, it was prepared the creation of the precursor form of the band. The first regulation, including 28 articles, was voted at a meeting at the Saturday night on June 21, a meeting which should be considered as the establishment of the new club.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-Kpm.
Music Museum of the Corfu Philharmonic Society
Αρχείο και Μουσείο Φιλαρμονικής Εταιρείας Κερκύρας
Corfu Philharmonic Society Archive and Museum
The Corfu Philharmonic Society was founded in 1840 and operates ever since to support the study of music and culture of the Ionian Islands. Its Museum accordingly was founded in 2010.

Κ. Καρδάμης. Έξι μελέτες για τη Φιλαρμονική Εταιρεία Κερκύρας στα «Δημοσιεύματα του Μουσείου Μουσική 'Νικόλαος Χαλικιόπουλος Μάντζαρος'» (Κέρκυρα: Φιλαρμονική Εταιρεία Κερκύρας. 2010). ISBN: 978-960-99715-0-8

Κ. Καρδάμης. «To Μουσείο και Αρχείο της Φιλαρμονικής Εταιρείας Κερκύρας». ανακοινωση στο 1ο Συνέδριο Μουσικών Βιβλιοθηκών & Αρχείων του Ελληνικού Παραρτήματος της IAML (Αθήνα. Μεγάλη Μουσική Βιλιοθήκη της Ελλάδος 'Λίλιαν Βουδούρη'. 21-22.4.2017)
«Ο Διονύσιος Ροδοθεάτος και τα συμφωνικά έργα του». Μουσικός Ελληνομνήμων 1 (Σεπτέμβριος–Δεκέμβριος 2008). 18–23
«"Ερχόντουν η μουζική της φιλαρμονικής μέσα εις ένα μπατέλο": Μουσική και πολιτική στην Κέρκυρα του 1850». Μουσικός Ελληνομνήνων 18-19 (Μάιος-Δεκέμβριος 2014). 3-23
«"Ερχόντουν η μουζική της φιλαρμονικής μέσα εις ένα μπατέλο": Μουσική και πολιτική στην Κέρκυρα του 1850». Μουσικός Ελληνομνήνων 18-19 (Μάιος-Δεκέμβριος 2014). 3-23
Καίτη Ρωμανού κ.α.. Η μουσική βιβλιοθήκη της Φιλαρμονικής Εταιρείας Κερκύρας. Κουλτούρα. Αθήνα 2004.

Kostas Kardamis, «The music of the Ionian Islands and its contribution to the emergence of "Greek National Music"», Anthony Hirst, Patrick Sammon (επιμ.), The Ionian Islands. Aspects of their History and Culture (Newcastle upon Tyne: Cambridge Scholars Publishing, 2014), 340-366
Online map with music collections in Greece. AMA WG GR IAML

The Skriperos Philharmonic Association was founded on May 3, 1909 in the village of Skriperos, located 18 km NW of the city of Corfu. It is the fourth band in a row, out of the 20 in total that operate on the island. The purpose of its establishment was and is to provide free music education to young people in the area and their participation with the Philharmonic in cultural events of wider interest. Since its foundation, it has participated in a number of social, cultural, religious and educational events, with an average of 50 public appearances annually with the band, bandina (youth band) and chamber music ensembles operating in the Philharmonic. A bi annual newsletter is published, promoting Association's actions.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-Kps.

Est in 1974 for “research, preservation, study, and presentation of the material culture of the Peloponnese as well as of the whole of Greece” Aside from the more than 10.000 objects of folk art (costumes, fabrics, utensils, furniture etc.) there are more than 70 musical instruments and bells, an important collection of audio-visual documents and other rare material. The Foundation publishes books, is issuing a series of Greek folk music recordings, and has produced documentary films. The scientific review “Ethnographika” features articles of musicological interest along with other folklore studies. Open to the public. , Library has 12.000 books and 250 periodical publications covering fields of Ethnograpfhy, Costume, Traditional Dance and Music.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-Npl.
Aristoteleio Panepistēmio Thessalonikēs,Tmēma Mousikōn Spoudōn, Vivliothēkē
Αριστοτέλειο Πανεπιστήμιο Θεσσαλονίκης, Τμήμα Μουσικών Σπουδών, Βιβλιοθήκη
Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, School of Musical Studies, Library

Exclusively music Library. Books on music, scores, audiovisual material, rare books, early prints, music MSS of the 20th cent (ca. 2.000 sheets of ecclesiastical and serious music). A Byzantine music parchment fragment of the 12th-13th cent and a 19th cent Byzantine Music MS (1 Papadikē) are on permanent loan to the Library by Aris Bazmadelis. Library contains MSS of 20th cent Greek composers of serious and ecclesiastical music (M. Kastellēs, M. Guinopoulos, N. Astrinidēs, N. Mavropoulos), as well as a large collection of traditional songs and thesis with music interest in the digital repository. The Library holds the traditional songs recordings collections of Panayiota-Hara Hristara (East Halkidiki 500 songs) and Athena Katsanevaki (Macedonia, Epirus, Crete, ca 12000 songs).
The former RISM library siglum was GR-THms.

Archival and other holdings: Archeio Byzantinōn Cheirographōn Nikolaou Mavropoulou, School of Music Studies, shelfmark LMS NM. Glory to the Lauds and stichera of Vespers and Matins of various Saints of the Eastern Orthodox Church are mostly included in the collection.

Online repository: The online collection of the music manuscripts collection of Nikolaos Mavropoulos, provides access to digitized versions of about 70 Byzantine music scores. http://www.mus.auth.gr/cms/?q=en/node/2478
Αριστοτέλειο Πανεπιστήμιο Θεσσαλονίκης, Θεολογική Σχολή, Βιβλιοθήκη
Aristotle University of Thessaloniki, Faculty of Theological Studies, Library
Library holds a collection of books on byzantine music, many of them of the 19th cent. and rare audiovisual material.
The former RISM library siglum was GR-THts.

Est in 1932, the collection covers all fields of knowledge. There are rare books (incuding incunabula), many of which were acquired with the purchase (in 1949 and 1952) of the private libraries of Alexandros Leukaditēs and Diogenous Dellēs. Music in genral collection. There are two music MSS (heirmologia), catalogued by Mastrodēmētrēs. He has dated one as before 1815 and the other after 1815. Open to the public daily except Sunday.

Founded in 1915 is the first music library opened to the public, in Greece. Holds important music collections of rare books, archives, programmes, photographs and manuscripts of composers and artists, as well as paper clipping and other ephemera associated with the city’s musical life. Manuscripts of Emile Riadis, Denis Visvardis, Loris Margaritis, Alexandre Kazantzi, Kostas Nikitas and many others, are kept in the library as well as an original manuscript of Manolis Kalomoiris. Besides the music collections of books and manuscripts, Library holds an impostant historic archive dating from 1914 to our days, as well as a remarkable collection of music instruments (folk and western).
Hiera Patriarchikē kai Stavropēgiakē Monē tōn Vlatadōn
Ιερά Πατριαρχική και Σταυροπηγιακή Μονή των Βλατάδων
Holy Patriarchal and Stavropegiac Monastery of Vlatades

The monastery was founded somewhere between 1351 to 1371 AD by the empress Anna Palaiologina. It preserves religious and historic relics that include several scripts and manuscripts, codecs and Turkish firmans, imperial golden bulls (imperial golden seals) and patriarchal sigilliums. A huge collection of portable Byzantine religious icons of great historic and artistic value is also kept and preserved within the Vlatadon monastery. The rare books and manuscripts of the monastery, including the musical ones, are kept in the Library of the Patriarchal Institute of Patristic Studies which is located within the courtyard of the monastery
Tsioumē, Chrysanthē. Vlatadon Monastery. Thessaloniki: Institute for Balkan Studies, 1987,
Ιερά Μονή Βλατάδων. Ιερά Βασιλική και Σταυροπηγιακή Μονή Βλατάδων. [Θεσσαλονίκη]: [Ι. Μονή Βλατάδων], 1999;
Stogioglou, Geōrgios A. Η εν Θεσσαλονίκη πατριαρχική μονή των Βλατάδων. Θεσσαλονίκη: Πατριαρχικόν Ιδρυμα Πατερικών Μελετών, 1971.
Ιερά Μονή Βλατάδων (Θεσσαλονίκη, Ελλάδα). Σκευοφυλάκιο Ιεράς Μονής Βλατάδων. Θεσσαλονίκη: Υπουργείο Πολιτισμού, 9η Εφορεία Βυζαντινών Αρχαιοτήτων, 1994,
Παναγιωτίδης. Π. "Η Έρευνα της Βυζαντινής Μουσικής Μέσα Από τα Αρχεία Στον Ελλαδικό Χώρο", 3ο Συνέδριο των Ελληνικών Μουσικών Βιβλιοθηκών και Αρχείων. Θεσσαλονίκη 10-11 Μαΐου 2019.
Online map of music librarues and collections AMA WG, GR IAML
Holy, Royal, Patriarchal and Stavropegic Monastery of the Vlatades, Eptapyrgiou 64
Patriarcal Institute of Patristic Studies, Library

The Institute holds two unique collections. The archive of manuscripts in microfilm copies from the Libraries of the Mount Athos Monasteries and the archive of slide copies of illustrated manuscripts of Mount Athos Monasteries, among them many music mss. Digitised manuscripts from the Skete of Kafsokalyvia in Mount Athos, Digital copies of the archives of the Monastery of Saint Anastasia in Thessaloniki area and of the Monastery of Saint Ignatius (Leimōnos) in Lesvos are also kept in the Archive.The Library numbers more than 50.000 vols with emphasis on theological field. 400 early books. Books on Byzantine Music in general collection. The Institute is active in publications related to the subject of research. In Byzantine music in particular, has so far published seven monographs, in the series "Ψαλτικά Βλατάδων" (Psaltika Vlatadon).
Παναγιωτίδης. Π. "Η Έρευνα της Βυζαντινής Μουσικής Μέσα Από τα Αρχεία Στον Ελλαδικό Χώρο", 3ο Συνέδριο των Ελληνικών Μουσικών Βιβλιοθηκών και Αρχείων. Θεσσαλονίκη 10-11 Μαΐου 2019.
Hiera Patriarchikē kai Stavropēgiakē Monē tōn Vlatadōn
Online map with music collections in Greece. AMA Project group of the Greek Branch of IAML

Η Μονή Μυρσινιδίου είναι ανδρικό ορθόδοξο μοναστήρι στη Χίο. Εκκλησιαστικώς ανήκει στη μητρόπολη Χίου, Ψαρών και Οινουσσών. Ιδρύθηκε το 1887 από τον ιερομόναχο Χριστοφόρο Σερέμελη, ο οποίος φύλαξε εκεί την Εικόνα της Παναγίας που βρήκε κοντά στη θάλασσα. Σύμφωνα με τον Richard Marcel, η Βιβλιοθήκη φυλάσσει μερικά μουσικά χειρόγραφα.

Moni Myrsinidiou (Myrsinidi Monastery) was founded by monk Christoforos Seremelis (1897). It is dedicated to Panagia Myrtidiotissa (Virgin of the Myrtles), after the miraculous icon, which the monk found in the sea that the monastery overlooks. The monastery celebrates on September 24 when crowds of worshippers come to honour Her Grace. According to Marcel Richard "... le monastaire posse derait quelques autres MSS, surtout musicaux"
Hegumen Archimandrite is Fr Nektarios Epitropakis.
